title: Gospel power and wrath against ungodliness
summary_text: Paul, servant of Jesus Christ, called apostle, writes to Rome’s believers about God’s gospel promised beforehand—the Son descended from David according to flesh, declared Son of God in power by resurrection. The gospel is God’s power for salvation to everyone believing, to Jew first and Greek; God’s wrath is revealed from heaven against ungodliness that suppresses truth. Gentiles knew God yet exchanged his glory for images; God gave them up to impurity, dishonorable passions, and a debased mind.
key_points: The gospel was promised through the prophets.
In it God’s righteousness is revealed from faith to faith.
God’s wrath is revealed against ungodliness and unrighteousness.
They knew God but did not glorify him as God.
God gave them up in the lusts of their hearts.
evidence_phrases: a servant of Jesus Christ
the gospel of God
it is the power of God for salvation
For the wrath of God is revealed
they exchanged the glory of the incorruptible God
God gave them up to the lusts of their hearts

title: Impartial judgment and circumcision of heart
summary_text: God’s judgment is according to truth against those who judge others while practicing the same. Kindness leads to repentance; stubbornness stores up wrath. There is no partiality with God; hearers of the law are not justified before doers. Gentiles without the law may show the law’s work on their hearts. True circumcision is of the heart in the Spirit, not merely letter and praise of men.
key_points: You are without excuse who judge another.
God will render to each according to his works.
There is no partiality with God.
Circumcision profits if you are a doer of the law.
He is not a Jew who is one outwardly only.
evidence_phrases: the judgment of God is according to truth
repentance
there is no partiality with God
not the hearers of the law are righteous
he is not a Jew who is one outwardly

title: All under sin; justification through faith
summary_text: What advantage has the Jew? Much—in every way, chiefly that they were entrusted with God’s oracles. Unbelief does not nullify God’s faithfulness. All have sinned; no one is justified by works of law. God’s righteousness is manifested apart from law, witnessed by the Law and Prophets—through faith in Jesus Christ to all who believe. Boasting is excluded; one God justifies circumcision by faith and uncircumcision through faith.
key_points: Jews were entrusted with the oracles of God.
There is no one righteous; all have sinned.
Being justified freely by his grace through redemption in Christ.
God set him forth as a propitiation by faith in his blood.
We maintain that a man is justified by faith apart from works of the law.
evidence_phrases: they were entrusted with the oracles of God
There is no one righteous
for all have sinned
being justified freely by his grace
God set him forth to be a propitiation
We maintain therefore that a man is justified by faith apart from the deeds of the law

title: Abraham justified by faith before circumcision
summary_text: Abraham believed God and it was counted to him for righteousness before circumcision, that he might be father of all who believe without being circumcised. The promise is sure to faith, not law, lest it be of works. Faith is counted as righteousness as David describes blessedness of whose sins are forgiven. Abraham is father of circumcised who walk in faith’s steps and father of Gentile believers. Against hope he believed in hope regarding descendants.
key_points: Abraham’s faith was counted for righteousness.
Righteousness is not of the law but of faith.
He is father of circumcision to those who walk in faith.
In hope he believed against hope.
He was fully convinced that what God promised he was able to perform.
evidence_phrases: Abraham believed God
his faith is counted for righteousness
the heir of the world
the promise is of faith
David also pronounces blessing
who is the father of us all
who in hope believed against hope

title: Peace, access, and Adam and Christ
summary_text: Having been justified by faith, we have peace with God through Christ and rejoice in hope of God’s glory—even glory in tribulations that produce endurance. God’s love is poured into hearts through the Holy Spirit. While we were weak, Christ died for the ungodly; while sinners, we were reconciled. Where sin increased, grace abounded more. As through one man sin and death entered, so through one man grace and gift abound to many.
key_points: We have peace with God through our Lord Jesus Christ.
Christ died for the ungodly at the right time.
We were reconciled while enemies.
Where sin abounded, grace abounded more.
As through one trespass all men were condemned, so through one act of righteousness all will be justified.
evidence_phrases: we have peace with God
Christ died for the ungodly
while we were enemies, we were reconciled
where sin abounded, grace abounded more
as through one man sin entered into the world

title: Buried and raised with Christ; slaves of righteousness
summary_text: Shall we continue in sin that grace may abound? We died to sin—how can we live in it? Baptized into Christ’s death, buried with him, raised to walk in newness of life. Old man crucified; freed from sin. Present yourselves to God as alive from the dead; instruments of righteousness. Once slaves of sin, now obey from the heart the teaching; fruit unto sanctification, end eternal life—wages of sin is death, God’s gift eternal life.
key_points: We died to sin; how can we live any longer in it?
We were buried with him through baptism into death.
Our old man was crucified with him.
Present yourselves to God as alive from the dead.
The wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life.
evidence_phrases: How can we, who died to sin, still live in it?
We were buried therefore with him
our old man was crucified with him
present yourselves to God, as alive from the dead
But now, being made free from sin
the wages of sin is death

title: Law, sin, and inner conflict
summary_text: The law speaks to those who know law; through law knowledge of sin. Dead to law through Christ’s body to belong to another. Sin produced coveting through commandment. Sin working in me slew me; the law is spiritual but I am flesh sold under sin. I delight in God’s law inwardly but see another law in members. Wretched man—who delivers? Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ.
key_points: Through the law comes the knowledge of sin.
Sin, finding occasion through the commandment, deceived me.
The law is spiritual, but I am fleshly.
I see a different law in my members.
Thanks be to God through Jesus Christ our Lord!
evidence_phrases: through the law comes the knowledge of sin
sin, finding occasion through the commandment
the law is spiritual, but I am fleshly
I see a different law in my members
Who will deliver me
I thank God through Jesus Christ our Lord!

title: Spirit life; heirs; nothing separates
summary_text: No condemnation for those in Christ Jesus who walk after the Spirit. Law of Spirit of life freed from sin and death. Those in flesh cannot please God; you are in Spirit if God’s Spirit dwells in you. Heirs with Christ if we suffer with him. Creation groans; we groan for adoption redemption. God works all things for good. Who shall separate us from Christ’s love?
key_points: There is therefore now no condemnation.
The law of the Spirit of life in Christ Jesus made me free.
You are not in the flesh but in the Spirit.
We are children of God, and if children, then heirs.
Who shall separate us from the love of Christ?
evidence_phrases: There is therefore now no condemnation
the law of the Spirit of life
the Spirit of God dwells in you
heirs of God, and joint heirs with Christ
the sufferings of this present time are not worthy to be compared
all things work together for good
Who shall separate us from the love of Christ?

title: Election, sovereignty, and mercy
summary_text: Paul has great sorrow for Israel’s unbelief; not all Israel are Israel. God’s word has not failed—children of promise are counted seed. God loved Jacob, hated Esau—not injustice; God has mercy on whom he will. Pharaoh raised up to show power. Vessels of wrath and mercy—Gentiles obtained righteousness Israel stumbled over. Stone of stumbling; whoever believes will not be disappointed.
key_points: Not all who are descended from Israel are Israel.
It is not the children of the flesh who are children of God.
I will have mercy on whom I have mercy.
So then it is not of him who wills, nor of him who runs, but of God who has mercy.
They stumbled over the stumbling stone.
evidence_phrases: not all who are descended from Israel are Israel
the children of the promise are counted as a seed
I will have mercy on whom I have mercy
So then it is not of him who wills
even us whom he also called, not from the Jews only
They stumbled over the stumbling stone

title: Confession, hearing, and Israel’s rejection
summary_text: Paul’s heart’s desire is Israel’s salvation; zeal without knowledge sought own righteousness. Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes. Confess with mouth Jesus is Lord, believe God raised him—saved. Faith comes by hearing; beautiful feet of gospel preachers. Israel did not obey; God stretched out hands to disobedient people.
key_points: They being ignorant of God’s righteousness sought to establish their own.
Christ is the end of the law for righteousness to everyone who believes.
If you will confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord
Faith comes by hearing, and hearing by the word of God.
All day long I spread out my hands to a disobedient people.
evidence_phrases: They being ignorant of God’s righteousness
Christ is the end of the law
if you will confess with your mouth
Whoever believes in him will not be disappointed
faith comes by hearing
Did they not hear? Yes, they did

title: Israel’s remnant and olive tree
summary_text: God has not cast off his people—Paul himself is a remnant chosen by grace, not works. Israel stumbled that Gentiles might be saved to provoke jealousy. Gentiles are wild olive branches grafted; boast not; fear God’s severity and kindness. All Israel will be saved as written—Deliverer from Zion; gifts and calling of God are irrevocable. God shut all up in disobedience to have mercy on all.
key_points: God has not rejected his people whom he foreknew.
There is also a remnant according to the election of grace.
If the root is holy, so are the branches.
You do not support the root, but the root supports you.
All Israel will be saved.
evidence_phrases: God has not rejected his people
a remnant according to the election of grace
if some of the branches were broken off
don’t be conceited
all Israel will be saved
the gifts and the calling of God are irrevocable
God has imprisoned them all in disobedience

title: Living sacrifice, gifts, and love
summary_text: Present bodies a living sacrifice; do not be conformed to world but transformed by renewing mind. Humble gifts in one body in Christ. Love without hypocrisy; abhor evil; fervent in spirit; bless persecutors; repay no evil; overcome evil with good.
key_points: Present your bodies a living sacrifice.
Do not be conformed to this world.
We have many members in one body.
Let love be without hypocrisy.
Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.
evidence_phrases: present your bodies a living sacrifice
don’t be conformed to this world
we, who are many, are one body in Christ
Let love be without hypocrisy
Bless those who persecute you
Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good

title: Authorities, love, and the day
summary_text: Let every soul be subject to higher authorities—there is no authority except from God. Rulers are not terror to good work. Love is fulfillment of law; do this knowing the time—salvation nearer; cast off darkness, put on armor of light.
key_points: There is no authority except from God.
Rulers are not a terror to the good work.
Love doesn’t harm a neighbor. Love therefore is the fulfillment of the law.
It is already time for you to awaken from sleep.
Put on the Lord Jesus Christ.
evidence_phrases: Let every soul be in subjection to the higher authorities
there is no authority except from God
rulers are not a terror to good work
love is therefore the fulfillment of the law
knowing the time
Put on the Lord Jesus Christ

title: Weak and strong; kingdom of God
summary_text: Receive weak in faith without disputes over doubtful things. Who are you who judge another’s servant? Each will stand before God. Kingdom is not eating/drinking but righteousness, peace, joy in Holy Spirit. Bear weaknesses of weak; please neighbor for good; accept one another as Christ accepted us.
key_points: Receive one who is weak in faith.
Who are you who judge another’s servant?
The Kingdom of God is not eating and drinking
We ought to bear the weaknesses of the weak.
Therefore accept one another, even as Christ also accepted you.
evidence_phrases: Now accept one who is weak in faith
Who are you who judges another’s servant?
the Kingdom of God is not eating and drinking
We ought to bear the weaknesses of the weak
Therefore let’s follow after things which make for peace
Therefore accept one another

title: Christ servant of circumcision; Gentile hope
summary_text: We ought to bear weaknesses of neighbors for edification. Christ became servant of circumcision for God’s truth and promises to fathers; Gentiles glorify God for mercy as Scriptures testify. Paul’s ministry—priestly offering of Gentiles acceptable, sanctified by Holy Spirit. Paul plans to visit Rome; prays for journey in God’s will.
key_points: Let each of us please his neighbor for good.
Now I say that Christ has been made a servant of the circumcision
Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy.
I have served as a priest the Good News of God.
I will come to you when I go to Spain.
evidence_phrases: Let each one of us please his neighbor for that which is good
Christ has been made a servant of the circumcision
the Gentiles might glorify God for his mercy
that I should be a servant of Christ Jesus to the Gentiles
when I go to Spain, I will come to you

title: Greetings and victory over Satan
summary_text: Commend Phoebe; greet Prisca, Aquila, and many co-workers. Watch those causing divisions; be wise to good, simple concerning evil. God of peace will crush Satan under feet shortly. Greetings from companions; glory to God.
key_points: I commend to you Phoebe our sister.
Greet Prisca and Aquila.
Note those who cause divisions.
The God of peace will quickly crush Satan under your feet.
To the only wise God be the glory forever.
evidence_phrases: I commend to you Phoebe our sister
Greet Prisca and Aquila
Now I beg you, brothers, look out for those
The God of peace will soon crush Satan under your feet
to the only wise God, through Jesus Christ, be the glory forever
